pattern: A segment tree over left endpoints with range updates tracks the distinct-parity difference so the longest balanced window is found in near-linear time.
hints: For a fixed right end, define for each left end the difference between distinct even and distinct odd counts of that window.
When the right end advances to a value v, that value newly appears only for left ends after its previous occurrence, so it adds plus or minus one to a contiguous range of left ends.

Maintain those differences in a segment tree with lazy range add; because neighboring differences change by at most one, you can descend to the leftmost index whose difference is zero and maximize the window length.

stress.description: moderate array over a narrow value range, sized to keep the build fast
stress.note: narrow range gives many duplicates and parity mixing

stress.description: wider value spread to test distinct tracking with few repeats
stress.note: sparse duplicates stress the segment tree differently
